heading: Discontinuance of agents, subagents, and interpreters
label: 65 Discontinuance of agents, subagents, and interpreters
text: The Secretary of the Interior shall, under the direction of the President, cause to be discontinued the services of such agents, subagents, interpreters, and mechanics as may from time to time become unnecessary, in consequence of the emigration of the Indians, or other causes.
notes: CodificationR.S. § 2073 derived from , .act July 9, 1832, ch. 174, § 54 Stat. 564
Indian AgentsThe services of Indian agents have been dispensed with. See note set out under .section 64 of this title

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions of other officers, employees, and agencies of Department of the Interior, with certain exceptions, to Secretary of the Interior, with power to delegate, see Reorg. Plan No. 3 of 1950, §§ 1, 2, eff. , 15 F.R. 3174, , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.May 24, 195064 Stat. 1262
